name: Birth Certificate
description: Apply for a Rwandan birth certificate, provided by the Ministry of Local Government (MINALOC) and processed by local authorities at the sector level. The service costs 500 RWF and is usually processed within 1 working day. The application is made online via IremboGov, and the certificate is issued as a downloadable e-certificate with lifetime validity. It can be requested for oneself or on behalf of a child.

requirements.name: National ID or Citizen Application Number
requirements.description: The applicant's National ID number, or a Citizen Application Number for those without a National ID (such as a child). The system uses this to retrieve the applicant's registered details.

requirements.name: Valid phone number
requirements.description: An active phone number used to receive the billing number and the notification when the certificate is ready.

requirements.name: Email address
requirements.description: An email address used for notifications and, optionally, to receive the issued certificate.

steps.description: Log in to IremboGov and select the Birth Certificate service, choosing whether the application is for yourself or a child.
Enter the National ID or Citizen Application Number; the registered details are retrieved automatically.
Fill in the processing details, the reason for the request, and select the sector office.
Verify the information, submit the application, and receive a billing number (starting with 88).
Pay the 500 RWF fee using a supported method such as Mobile Money.
Once the sector Executive Secretary approves it, download the birth certificate from IremboGov.
